items.title: Trump singles out Australia as he criticises allies on Iran war support

children.content: The US president singles out Australia as a country he was surprised was unwilling to help secure the Strait of Hormuz amid the war with Iran.

articles.title: German defence minister says Trump has 'no exit strategy' in Iran

children.content: Boris Pistorius says Germany will only help to secure the Strait of Hormuz once the US and Iran strike a ceasefire deal.

image.altText: Boris Pistorius

articles.title: In southern Lebanon, conflict with Israel has become an 'existential war'

children.content: Southern Lebanon has a "very long history" of conflict with Israel, and many of its residents remember past wars clearly. But they say the latest incursion feels different.

image.altText: A man leans against a railing, looking thoughtful.

articles.title: States to seek consistent reporting of fuel shortages at national cabinet

children.content: State and territory leaders are also expected to push for a national approach to any further measures, including fuel rationing, if supply were to run short.

image.altText: Two men and a woman in suits stand at a podium speaking to journalists with the Australian flag in the background.

items.title: Trump reveals Iran's 'great gift', plus what else you should know this morning

image.altText: A man wearing a helmet and flak jacket with the word PRESS on it looks at the camera with building rubble behind him

children.content: Mr Trump is now sceptical at the prospect of a peace deal soon, just days after saying there were positive signs for peace. Middle East correspondent Matthew Doran has your daily update on the Middle East conflict.

items.title: Is the end of the Iran war near?

children.content: Donald Trump says Iran wants a ceasefire deal. But Tehran says it's not even talking with the US. So what's going on and how likely is a ceasefire?

image.altText: Donald Trump looking up in an inquisitive manner

items.title: Trump extends deadline for Iran to open Strait of Hormuz, takes swipe at Australia

children.content: US President Donald Trump singles out Australia while accusing a string of allies of failing to help secure the Strait of Hormuz, before extending Iran’s’ deadline to open the channel. Follow live.

image.altText: Strait of Hormuz pic

images.altText: A satellite image of a cyclone with a well-defined eye and a large swirl of clouds.

articles.title: 'You are in danger': Residents told to take shelter as Cyclone Narelle closes in

children.content: Residents on WA's West Pilbara coast are feeling the full force of Tropical Cyclone Narelle as it travels south as a category four system.

image.altText: A tracking map showing a storm system curling south.

children.content: The storm, which has already made landfall in Far North Queensland and the Northern Territory, is now impacting the west Pilbara coast.

items.title: 'Monster' Cyclone Narelle to cross WA coast later today

updates.title: WA's Mid West 'taking very cautious approach'
Cyclone 'sounds like an aeroplane taking off'

items.title: Marles defends Australia's Middle East contribution after Trump's comments

children.content: Deputy Prime Minister Richard Marles has defended Australia's contribution to the defence of Gulf states after US President Donald Trump's comments. Follow live.

image.altText: Close up of Richard Marles wearing a suit and tie.

items.title: Superannuation giant HESTA's new admin provider on brink of collapse

children.content: Experts are concerned one of Australia's largest super funds has invested in a new admin company whose most recent financial report indicates it is insolvent.

image.altText: HESTA logo on a wall

items.title: IOC bans transgender athletes from women's events

children.content: Under changes announced by the International Olympic Committee, the gender of athletes will be determined by a one-time gene-screening test.

image.altText: Weightlifter Laurel Hubbard drops the bar behind herself as she competes in front of the Olympic rings.

items.title: Australian dependence on imported packaging exposed by Iran war

children.content: The war in Iran renews the government's interest in reducing Australia's dependence on imported packaging, particularly oil-reliant plastic.

image.altText: Bunches of apples and avocados wrapped in plastic and fruit netting in the supermarket

items.title: Katherine's worst flood in 28 years leaves survivors with disaster 'PTSD'

children.content: As it recovers from its worst flooding in decades, those living in the NT town of Katherine are trying to come to terms with the ongoing threat on their doorstep.

gallery.altText: A man stands next to a fence covered in mud.
Three men look at the wall of a house.
A person wearing a camoflague uniform lifts rubbish into a skip bin.

thumbnail.altText: A man stands next to a fence covered in mud.
Three men look at the wall of a house.
A person wearing a camoflague uniform lifts rubbish into a skip bin.

items.title: 'There is still hope': Former detective reflects six months on from Gus's disappearance

children.content: Six months to the day since four-year-old Gus Lamont was reported missing from a vast outback station, many more questions than answers remain.

image.altText: Composite image of missing boy Gus Lamont imposed on an image of the vast outback with a group of police.

items.title: Fuel not the only political problem posed by Middle East war

children.content: Fuel prices are the immediate issue for the Albanese government but it will also be under scrutiny for how it handles Australia's relationship with the United States and its volatile president.

image.altText: Chris Bowen press conference

items.title: Soaring diesel prices hit roads, housing and infrastructure builds

children.content: Industry groups have warned some infrastructure projects could stall without additional government support to cover fuel-related costs.

image.altText: Construction workers in high visibility clothing working on a construction site.

items.title: 'Reliable evidence': Exercise halves early death risk of middle-aged women

children.content: Australian researchers found that middle-aged women who exercised regularly halved their risk of premature death. But most women aren't meeting physical activity guidelines.

image.altText: Woman jogging while listening music at beach against sky

items.title: Weekly news quiz: Jet fuel shortages, headbutting whales and Donald Trump's social faux pas

children.content: It's been a big week in the news. Did you catch these stories?

items.title: Grey's Anatomy marks 21 years of love, loss and disasters

children.content: Twenty-one years ago today, a group of interns walked into the fictional Seattle Grace Hospital to start their surgical careers. It was the start of the longest-running medical drama in US TV history.

image.altText: A woman in blue scrubs and a white coat looking concerned.

items.title: Nazeem Hussain developed his 'gut-punch' comedy over a lifetime of racism

children.content: For comedian Nazeem Hussain, the best way to respond to war, discrimination and racism is to laugh about it. And he's built his whole career on this special brand of risky, outrageous comedy.

image.altText: Actor Nazeem Hussain stands smiling on a beach at sunset

items.title: Craig's experience of abuse is not uncommon — but it is seldom talked about

children.content: Craig Hughes-Cashmore spent more than 20 years living with persistent suicidal thoughts, an experience that many share but rarely feel able to discuss.

items.title: Complaints about foul smells skyrocket in Melbourne's west

children.content: Adam Pattison spent two years doing up his backyard to enjoy outdoor barbecues, but says "sickening" smells kill the vibe five times a week.

image.altText: Adam Pattison looking annoyed in his backyard

items.title: Severe storm causes thousands of power outages, knocks out transport

children.content: Fire and Rescue NSW says there has been more than 400 calls for help, including one incident where a tree fell on a house trapping the occupant.

image.altText: A flooded road in the late afternoon with cars driving through with headlights on.

items.title: Petition calls for video of death in custody to be released despite coroner ruling

children.content: Raylene Nixon says the public should be able to make their own minds up about what happened to her son in his final moments.

image.altText: Man in yellow shirt with cap stands in front of Aboriginal painting.

items.title: ASIC expands in NT after investigation into Indigenous customers' bank fees

children.content: The chair of the Australian Securities and Investments Commission says the expansion of the watchdog agency in Darwin will help the organisation better enforce regulations in remote communities.

image.altText: Shoulder-up shot of a white man, gray hair, wearing light blue collared shirt, light-framed glasses, neutral expression

items.title: The One Nation vote in SA

children.content: One Nation just sent a shiver through the Liberal Party, after a breakthrough result in South Australia.
Pauline Hanson's party achieved more than 21 per cent of the statewide primary vote, beating the Liberal Party and becoming the second-highest polling party.
But nationally, more attention has been on the undercard
fight, and that has left the Liberal Party deeply wounded.

image.altText: TN_OneNation_Loop

items.title: More than 250 voters in Coober Pedy unable to have their say in SA election

children.content: Nearly one in three Coober Pedy voters, or more than 250 people, missed their chance to vote in the state election last week after the town was left without a polling booth on election day.

image.altText: An aerial view of the township of Coober Pedy

items.title: Second 'crew' charged over Sydney woman's kidnapping, murder

children.content: Further charges have been laid over the alleged kidnapping and murder of a western Sydney woman who police believe was targeted due to her husband's organised crime links.

image.altText: Thi Kim Tran in a selfie photo, with a purple background, smiling.

items.title: Cherry-Evans's return to Brookvale a reminder of Manly's past amid an uncertain future

children.content: With Manly's season teetering on the edge, the Sea Eagles faithful welcomed former skipper Daly Cherry-Evans to Brookvale Oval in enemy colours for the first time. But it was the prodigal son who won the day.

image.altText: Daly Cherry-Evans shakes hands with a Manly player

items.title: Max Verstappen kicks Guardian F1 reporter out of Japanese Grand Prix press conference

children.content: Max Verstappen refuses to speak to media until Guardian journalist Giles Richards leaves because of a question he asked last year.

image.altText: Max Verstappen talks to a reporter.

items.title: Offshore wind power to save billions of dollars and kilometres of powerlines

children.content: Developing Victoria's wind power sector could save the state money and powerline infrastructure, while providing stability for Eastern Australia's power grid, a new report shows.

gallery.altText: Turbines in an offshore wind farm in the ocean.

thumbnail.altText: Turbines in an offshore wind farm in the ocean.

items.title: Climate groups worry 'business as usual' will sink Tasmania's net zero status

children.content: Tasmania loves to boast about its net zero emissions. But a new report has found that status could be "temporary" if further actions are not taken.

image.altText: Kelp forest in ocean.

items.title: How 'traditional' gender roles are making a comeback in modern society

children.content: It turns out generation Z has some of the strongest traditional beliefs about gender roles. The revival of what many see as outdated gender norms is being attributed to "manosphere" content on social media.

image.altText: A family of two children, a man and a woman at a table. The left half is black and white; the right is red and white.

items.title: 'Darkest day': Police to mark 40th anniversary of Melbourne's Russell Street bombing

children.content: The 1986 car bomb attack on Victoria Police headquarters in Melbourne claimed the life of 21-year-old constable Angela Taylor and injured more than 20 others.

image.altText: The fiery wreckage of a car burns outside the police headquarters in Russell Street, in a still taken from file video footage.

items.title: Advocates say declining organ donation rate a 'national disgrace'

children.content: Vanessa Hujber did not think twice before donating her kidney to save the life of her daughter, Amelia. But Amelia was one of the lucky ones, with advocates calling for urgent action to arrest Australia's declining living organ donation rate.

image.altText: Woman with brown hair and white top kissing woman with brown hair and brown top

items.title: Hundreds of patients finally have a GP as clinic reopens its books

children.content: A medical centre in Central West NSW is accepting new patients for the first time in 18 months, after community support helped attract six new doctors.

image.altText: Six doctors standing in a medical centre

items.title: Nicolás Maduro appears in court as Trump warns of more charges

children.content: Former Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro and his wife return to the New York courtroom after being captured by US forces in January.

image.altText: Nicolas Maduro being perp walked by multiple heavily armed police.

items.title: AFL concussion class action expands to more than 100 former players

children.content: Eight more footballers have joined a multimillion-dollar concussion class action against the AFL, tipping the total to over 100 former players who all claim they have been left brain-damaged by the game.

articles.title: AFL says new illicit drug policy is 'most stringent in Australian sport'

children.content: The AFL says its new illicit drugs policy, which includes biannual hair-testing of all players, is the most stringent in Australian sport.

image.altText: Darcy Moore and Andrew Dillon at a press conference

articles.title: Ryswyk says reaction to coming out has been 'overwhelming'

children.content: Former Brisbane Lions player Leigh Ryswyk says the support he received after becoming the first AFL player to come out as gay has "blown me away".

gallery.altText: Max Rooke kicks during an Australian Rules football match.

thumbnail.altText: Max Rooke kicks during an Australian Rules football match.
